titleStmt.title: SII 1.35: original edition by Eugen Hultzsch
PART I. SANSKRIT INSCRIPTIONS. II. COPPER-PLATE GRANTS OF THE EASTERN CHALUKYA DYNASTY. No. 35. A GRANT OF NARENDRA-Mل¹ڑIGARؤ€JA.

summary.p: The subjoined five grants belong to the kings Narendra-mل¹›igarؤپja or Vijayؤپditya II, Amma I. or Vishل¹‡uvardhana VI, Chؤپlukya-Bhؤ«ma II. or Vishل¹‡uvardhana VII, Amma II. or Vijayؤپditya V. and Vؤ«ra-Choل¸چa or Vishل¹‡uvardhana IX. The place, which is occupied by each of these princes in the genealogy of the Eastern Chalukya dynasty, will be seen from the annexed table, for which all hitherto published Eastern Chalukya grants have been consulted, and in which numbers are prefixed to the names of those princes who really reigned, in order to mark their succession. For previous lists of the Eastern Chalukya dynasty, see Sir Walter Elliot's Numismatic Gleanings, No. 2, in the Madras Journal, New Series, Vol. IV, pp. 81 f. and the same scholar's Coins of Southern India, p. 87; Dr. Burnell's South-Indian Palaeography, pp. 21 f. and Sewell's Lists of Antiquities, Vol. II, p. 152.

The relation of the two usurpers (18) Tؤپlapa and (21) Yuddhamalla to the direct line of the family is established by three inscriptions:â€”a. Tؤپل¸چapa is called the son of Vikramؤپditya's brother (Ind. Ant. Vol. XIV. p. 56); b. Tؤپla is called the son of Yuddhamalla, who was the paternal uncle of Chؤپlukya-Bhؤ«ma I. (Ind. Ant. Vol. XIII, p. 249, where pitل¹›ivya has to be read for pitل¹›ivyo); c. Bhؤ«ma II, the son of Kollabhigaل¹‡ل¸چa Vijayؤپditya, is at the same time called the son of Yuddhamalla, the son of Tؤپlapa, i.e., he belonged to the next generation after (21) Yuddhamalla (Ind. Ant. Vol. XII, p. 92).
Three of the last kings, who are shown in the annexed table, viz., (28) Vijayؤپditya VI, (29) Rؤپjarؤپja II. and (30) Vؤ«ra-Choل¸چa, are only known from the subjoined inscription No. 39.

This grant belongs to the Sir W. Elliot Collection of the British Museum, and was made over to me for publication by Dr. Burgess. It consists of five copper-plates with raised rims. Each plate measures 9 by 3 inches. The first and fifth plates are inscribed only on their inner sides, while the three middle ones bear writing on both sides. The preservation of the plates is tolerably good. They are strung on an elliptic ring, which is (1/2)" thick and 4(7/8)" by 3(1/2)" in diameter. The well-preserved circular seal, which is attached to the ring, measures 2(5/8)" in diameter. It bears the sun and the moon at the top, the legend إ›rؤ«tribhuvanؤپل¹پkuإ›a across the centre, and an expanded lotus-flower (side-view) at the bottomâ€”all in relief on a counter-sunk surface.

The document is a grant of the parama-mؤپheإ›vara Narendra-mل¹›igarؤپja, alias Vijayؤپditya II., the son of Vishل¹‡uvardhana IV. and grandson of Vijayؤپditya I. The name of the district (vishaya), to the inhabitants of which the king addresses his order, is lost. On the occasion of a lunar eclipse (chandra-grahaل¹‡a-nimitteCompare uttarؤپyaل¹‡animitte in No. 37, and Yؤپjأ±avalkya, I, 203: dؤپtavyaل¹پ pratyahaل¹پ pؤپtre nimitteل¹£u viإ›eل¹£ataل¸¥, where the Mitؤپksharؤپ explains nimitteل¹£u by candroparؤپgؤپdiل¹£u. The nimittas are specified in an inscription from Dholpur, which I have published in the Zeitschr. d. Deutsch. Morg. Gesellschaft, Vol. XL, p. 38, where king Chaل¹‡ل¸چamahؤپsena is said to have made gifts rؤپhإ«parodhaparvaل¹‡i, saل¹پkrؤپntau and ayanؤپdؤپل¹پ.) the king gave the village of Koل¹ںل¹ںapaل¹ںل¹ںu to twenty-four brؤپhmaل¹‡as. Of these, six adhered to the Hiraل¹‡yakeإ›i-sإ«tra and eighteen to the ؤ€pastamba-sإ«tra. They belonged to the following gotل¹›as:â€”Agniveإ›ya, Kauل¹‡ل¸چinya, Kauإ›ika, Gautama, Parؤپإ›ara, Bhؤپradvؤپja, Vatsa, إڑؤپل¹‡ل¸چilya, Saل¹پkل¹›iti and Harita. According to the colophon of the grant, â€œthe excellent prince Nل¹›ipa-Rudra, who was the brother of Narendra-mل¹›igarؤپja and a descendant of the Haihaya-vaل¹پإ›a (!), (was) the executor of this charity.â€‌ؤ€jأ±aptir asya dharmasya; ؤپjأ±apti, which occurs in several other grants, is evidently a synonym of dإ«taka â€œ(royal) messenger;â€‌ see Mr. Fleet's note 37, Ind. Ant. Vol. VIII, p. 79. In other inscriptions we find ؤپjأ±ؤپ (Vol. XIII, p. 122, note 45) and ؤپjأ±ؤپdؤپpaka (Vol. XIV, p. 161, note 28).

[[genealogical table:]] PEDIGREE OF THE EASTERN CHALUKYA DYNASTY. [C1]Kؤ«rtivarman (until إڑaka 489). [C1]Satyؤپإ›raya Vallabha (from إڑaka 532 until at least 556).See Mr. Fleet's Kanarese Dynasties, page 23. [C2]1. Kubja Vishل¹‡uvardhana I. VishamasiddhiThe surname Vishamasiddhi is found on the seals of some grants and in a grant published in Dr. Burnell's South-Indian Palaeography, 2nd edition, page 137, plate xxvii, line 4. See also Ind. Ant. Vol. VII, p. 185. (18 years; cir. إڑaka 526-27 to cir. 544-45). [C1]2. Jayasiل¹پha I. Vallabha (33 years; cir. إڑaka 544-45 to cir. 577-78).30 years according to Ind. Ant. Vol. VII, page 16; Vol. XIV, page 56. [C2]3. Indra Bhaل¹­ل¹­ؤپraka.According to the three latest inscriptions (Ind. Ant. XIV, 52, 56 and No. 39) he ruled 7 days. [C1]4. Vishل¹‡uvardhana II. (9 years; cir. إڑaka 577-78 to cir. 586-87.) [C1]5. Maل¹…gi-yuvarؤپja (25 years; cir. إڑaka 586-87 to cir. 611-12). [C1]6. Jayasiل¹پha II. (13 years; cir. إڑaka 611-12 to cir. 624-25.) [C2]7. Kokkili (6 months; cir. إڑaka 625). [C3]8. Vishل¹‡uvardhana III. (37 years; cir. إڑaka 625 to cir. 662.) [C1]9. Vijayؤپditya I. Bhaل¹­ل¹­ؤپraka (18 years; cir. إڑaka 662 to cir. 680). [C1]10. Vishل¹‡uvardhana IV. (36 years; cir. إڑaka 680 to cir. 716.) [C1]11. Vijayؤپditya II. Narendra-mل¹›igarؤپja (48 years; cir. إڑaka 716 to cir. 764).40 years according to No. 37 and Ind. Ant. VIII, 77; 44 years according to No. 36. [C1]12. Kali Vishل¹‡uvardhana V. (1(1/2) years; cir. إڑaka 764 to cir. 765-66.) [C1]13. Guل¹‡aga, Guل¹‡agؤپل¹…ka or Guل¹‡akenalla Vijayؤپditya III. (44 years; cir. إڑaka 765-66 to cir. 809-10.) [C2]Yuvarؤپja Vikramؤپditya. [C3]Yuddhamalla. [C1]14. Chؤپlukya-Bhؤ«ma I. Drohؤپrjuna (30 years; cir. إڑaka 809-10 to cir. 839-40.) [C1]15. Kollabhigaل¹‡ل¸چa, Kollabigaل¹‡ل¸چa or Kaliyarttyaل¹…ka Vijayؤپditya IV. (6 months; cir. إڑaka 840.) [C1]16. Amma I. Vishل¹‡uvardhana VI. Rؤپjamahendra (7 years; cir. إڑaka 840 to cir. 847). [C1]17. Vijayؤپditya.He reigned (1/2) month according to No. 37 and Ind. Ant. XIII, 249. [C1]18. Tؤپha, Tؤپla, Tؤپل¸چapa, Tؤپlapa or Tؤپل¸»apa (1 month; cir. إڑaka 847). [C1]19. Vikramؤپditya (11 months or 1 year; cir. إڑaka 847 to cir. 848). [C2]20. Bhؤ«ma.[[missing]] [C1]21. Yuddhamalla (7 years; cir. إڑaka 848 to cir. 855).He is left out altogether in No. 37 and Ind. Ant. XIII, 249. [C1]22. Chؤپlukya-Bhؤ«ma II. Vishل¹‡uvardhana VII. Gaل¹‡ل¸چamahendra, son of queen Meل¸»ؤپmbؤپ (12 years; cir. إڑaka 855 to 867). [C1]23. Amma II. Vijayؤپditya V. son of queen Lokamahؤپdevؤ«, ascended the throne in إڑaka 867[[missing]] and reigned 25 years (to cir. إڑaka 892). [C1]24. Dؤپnؤپrل¹‡ava or Dؤپna-nل¹›ipa (3 years; cir. إڑaka 892 to cir. 895). [C1]25. Aftor an interregnum of 27 years,[[missing]] إڑaktivarman or Chؤپlukyachandra reigned 12 years; circa إڑaka 925 to circa 937. [C2]26. Vimalؤپditya married Kإ«ndavؤپ, daughter of Rؤپjarؤپja of the Sإ«rya-vaل¹پإ›a and younger sister of Rؤپjendra-Choل¸چa (7 years; cir. إڑaka 937 to 944). [C1]27. Rؤپjarؤپja I. Vishل¹‡uvardhana VIII. married Ammaل¹…ga-devؤ«, daughter of Rؤپjendra-Choل¸چa of the Sإ«rya-vaل¹پإ›a, ascended the throne in إڑaka 944[[missing]] and reigned 41 years (to إڑaka 985). [C2]28. Vijayؤپditya VI. received the kingdom of Veل¹…gؤ« from his nephew Rؤپjendra-Choل¸چa and reigned 15 years (إڑaka 985 to 1000). [C1]Rؤپjendra-Choل¸چa, alias Kulottuل¹…ga-Choل¸چa-deva I, Kulottuل¹…ga-deva or Rؤپjanؤپrؤپyaل¹‡a, Choل¸چa king, married Madhurؤپntakؤ«, daughter of Rؤپjendradeva of the Sإ«rya-vaل¹پإ›a, and reigned 49 years (إڑaka 985 to 1034). [C1]Vikrama-Choل¸چa (15 years; إڑaka 1034 to 1049). [C2]29. Rؤپjarؤپja II. (1 year; إڑaka 1000 to 1001.) [C3]30. Vؤ«ra-Choل¸چa Vishل¹‡uvardhana IX. ascended the throne in إڑaka 1001; a grant[[missing]] is dated in the 21st year of his reign (إڑaka 1022). [C4]Four other sons. [C1]Kulottuل¹…ga-Choل¸چa-deva II. was reigning in إڑaka 1056.[[missing]]

revisionDesc.change: Further conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti . إ›rؤ«matؤپل¹پ sakalabhuvanasaل¹پstإ«yamؤپnamؤپnavyasagotrؤپل¹‡ؤپل¹پ hؤپritؤ«putrؤپل¹‡ؤپل¹پ
kauإ›ikؤ«varaprasؤپdalabdharؤپjyؤپnؤپل¹پ mؤپtل¹›gaل¹‡aparipؤپlitؤپnؤپل¹پ svؤپmimahؤپsenapؤپ
dؤپnudhyؤپtؤپnؤپل¹پ bhagavannؤپrؤپyaل¹‡aprasؤپdasamؤپsؤپditavaravarؤپhalؤپأ±chanekل¹£aل¹‡a
kل¹£aل¹‡avaإ›ؤ«kل¹›tؤپrؤپtimaل¹‡ل¸چalؤپnؤپ Amaإ›vamedhؤپvabhل¹›tthasnؤپnapavitrؤ«kل¹›tavapu
ل¹£ؤپل¹پ caل¸·ukyؤپnؤپل¹پ kulamalaل¹پkaripل¹‡oل¸¥ranekasamaramaل¹پghaل¹­ل¹­alabdhanijabhujavija
yaإ›rؤ«iyaل¸¥ niravadyodؤپraguل¹‡agaل¹‡ؤپlaل¹پkل¹›tasya إ›rؤ«vijayؤپdityamahؤپrؤپjasya
pautraل¸¥ sphuritakaravؤپل¸·adhؤپrؤپvaإ›ؤ«kل¹›tؤپrؤپtibhإ«maل¹‡ل¸چalasya svacaritanyakkل¹›ta
nل¹›ganaل¸·anahuل¹£ؤپل¹پvarؤ«ل¹£ayayؤپter viل¹£ل¹‡oriva svacakranandakasya viل¹£ل¹‡uvarddhanamahؤپ
rؤپjasya priyatanayaل¸¥ pratؤپpؤپnurؤپgؤپvanatasamastasؤپmantamaulؤ«lؤپل¸·itaإ›ؤپsanaل¸¥
kل¹£atrocitaإ›aktitrayapؤپtrؤ«bhإ«taل¸¥ Anekasaل¹پgrؤپmavijayؤپsؤپditavikramadhavaل¸·aل¸¥ ya
madaل¹‡ل¸چacaل¹‡ل¸چadorddaل¹‡ل¸چamaل¹‡ل¸چalؤپgranakhakhaل¹‡ل¸چitaripukarigaل¹‡ل¸چasthaل¸·eo narendramل¹›ga
rؤپjؤپaل¸¥ Arؤپtiل¹£aل¸چvargganigrahakaraل¸¥ samadhigatarؤپjؤپavidyؤپcatuل¹£ل¹­ayaل¸¥ caturupؤپyapra
yogacaturaل¸¥ duل¹£ل¹­ؤپanigrahaإ›iل¹£ل¹­ؤپnugrahakaraل¸¥ madhumathana Iva svavikramؤپkrؤپntabhuvanaل¸¥
yudhiل¹£ل¹­hira Iva bhؤ«mؤپrjunaparؤپkramasahؤپyaل¸¥ daإ›arathasuta Iva sؤ«tؤپnandanakaraل¸¥ manuri
va sanmؤپrggadarإ›إ›ؤ« padmؤپkؤپara Iva Aritimiranikaravidhvaل¹پsanؤپdityaل¸¥ paramabrahmaل¹‡yaل¸¥ paramamؤپhe
إ›varaل¸¥ samastabhإ«uvanؤپإ›rayaإ›rؤ«vijayؤپdityamahؤپrؤپjؤپdhirؤپjaparameإ›varabhaل¹­ل¹­ؤپrakaل¸¥
vؤپdiviل¹£ayanivؤپsino rؤپل¹£ل¹­rakإ«ل¹­ل¹­apramukhaل¸¥kإ«uل¹­uل¹پmbinaل¸¥ sarvvؤپnitthamؤپjأ±ؤپpayati . viditamastu
vaل¸¥ Abhipendaل¹‡ل¸چuruvؤپstavyؤپya kauإ›ikagotrؤپya hiraل¹‡yؤپakeإ›isإ«trؤپya veda
vedؤپل¹پgapaؤپragؤپya veل¹‡amaإ›armmaل¹‡e nalإ«cerivؤپstavyؤپya kauإ›ikagotrؤپya hi
raل¹‡yakeإ›isإ«trؤپya caل¹­iإ›armmaل¹‡e podeل¹پguvؤپstavyؤپya gautamagotrؤپya hiraل¹‡ya
keإ›isإ«trؤپya vidaإ›armmaل¹‡e podaل¹پguvؤپstavyؤپya saل¹پkrؤ«ل¹›ti gotrؤپya hiraل¹‡yؤپakeإ›isإ«
trؤپya maviل¹‡ل¸چiإ›armmaل¹‡e podeل¹پguvؤپstavyaؤپya Aharitagotrؤپya hiraل¹‡yؤپakeإ›isإ«trؤپya yؤپ
jأ±aإ›armmaل¹‡e podeل¹پguvaؤپstavyؤپya saل¹پkriل¹›tigotrؤپya hiraل¹‡yؤپakeإ›isإ«trؤپya إ›armma
ل¹‡e krovؤپإ›irivaؤپstavyؤپya Aharitagotrؤپya Apastambasإ«trؤپya kؤپmaإ›armma
ل¹‡e Urpuل¹­إ«ruvaؤپstavyؤپya bhؤپradvؤپjagotrؤپya Apastambasإ«trؤپya
viل¹£ل¹‡uإ›armmaل¹‡e vaل¹پgipaل¹ںل¹ںuvaؤپstavyؤپya kauل¹‡ل¸چinyagotrؤپya Apastambambasإ«trؤپ
ya guأ±jadevaإ›armmaل¹‡e vaل¹پgipaل¹ںل¹ںuvaؤپstavyؤپya إ›ؤپل¹‡ل¸چilyagotrؤپya Apa
stambasإ«trؤپya bhadraإ›armmaل¹‡e vaل¹پgipaل¹ںل¹ںuvaؤپstavyؤپya kauل¹‡ل¸چinyagotrؤپya Apa
stambasإ«trؤپya viل¹£ل¹‡uإ›armmaل¹‡e vaل¹پgipaل¹ںل¹ںuvaؤپstavyؤپya kauل¹‡ل¸چinyagotrؤپya A
pastambasإ«trؤپya nؤپrؤپyaل¹‡aإ›armmaل¹‡e cؤپnturuvaؤپstavyؤپya bhؤپradvؤپjagotrؤپ
ya Apastambasإ«trؤپya droل¹‡aإ›armmaل¹‡e cؤپnturuvaؤپstavyؤپya bhؤپradvؤپ
jagotrؤپya ؤ€pastambasإ«trؤپya nؤپrؤپyaل¹‡aإ›armmaل¹‡e krovaإ›irivaؤپstavyؤپ
ya haritagotrؤپya ؤ€pastambasإ«trؤپya mؤپdhavaإ›armmaل¹‡e krovaإ›irivaؤپsta
vyaؤپya parasؤپإ›aragotraؤپya Apastambasإ«trؤپya vennamaإ›armmaل¹‡e krovaإ›irivaؤپstavyؤپya vatsago
trؤپya Apastambasإ«trؤپya Arudiإ›armmaل¹‡e Urpuل¹­إ«ruvaؤپstavyؤپya bhؤپradvؤپjagotrؤپya
Apastambasإ«trؤپya nandiإ›armmaل¹‡e kؤپrahaiduvaؤپstavyؤپya bhؤپradvؤپjagotrؤپya Apa
stambasإ«trؤپya viل¹£ل¹‡uإ›armmaل¹‡e kؤپrahaiduvaؤپstavyؤپya bhؤپradvؤپjagotrؤپya Apastambasإ«trؤپ
ya bhؤپramؤپإ›armmaل¹‡e kؤپrahaiduvaؤپstavyؤپya bhؤپradvؤپjagotrؤپya Apastambasإ«trؤپya yaأ±jaإ›a
rmmaل¹‡e cؤپnturuvaؤپstavyؤپya bhؤپradvؤپjagotrؤپya Apastambasإ«trؤپya bؤپdadiإ›armmaل¹‡e krؤپja
vaؤپstavyؤپya kauل¹‡ل¸چinyagotrؤپya Apastambasإ«trؤپya veل¹‡ل¹‡aإ›armmaل¹‡e rؤپyإ«ruvaؤپstavyؤپ
ya Agnؤ«iveإ›yagotrؤپya Apastambasإ«trؤپya ل¹ںompayaإ›armmaل¹‡e vedavedؤپل¹پgaratebhyaل¸¥
ل¹£aل¹­karmmaniratebhyaإ› catruurviل¹پإ›ؤپtibrؤپhmaل¹‡ebhyؤپaإ› candruagrahaل¹‡animitte Udakapإ«
rvvavaل¹پ koل¹ںل¹ںapaل¹ںل¹ںu nؤپma grؤپmassarvvakaraparihؤپraل¹پ kل¹›tvؤپ dattaل¸¥ . Asyؤپvadhivicaؤپraل¸¥ pإ«rvvataل¸¥ A
tإ«gupaل¹ںل¹ںu dakل¹£iل¹‡ataل¸¥ vؤپnapaل¹ںل¹ںu paإ›cimataل¸¥ vؤپل¹‡ل¸چhaل¹ںإ«pedayإ« Uttarataل¸¥ ganayyؤپra
bu caturviإ›atyai dattaل¸¥ . Asyopari na kenacidbؤپdhؤپ karaل¹‡ؤ«yؤپ . karoti yassa pa
أ±camahؤپpؤپtakasaل¹پyukto bhavati . vyؤپsenؤپpyuktaل¹پ . bahubhirvvasudhؤپ dattؤپ bahubhiإ›cؤپnu
pؤپlitؤپ . yasya yasya yadؤپ bhإ«mistasya tasya tadؤپ phalaل¹پ . svadؤپattؤپل¹پ paradattؤپل¹پ vؤپ yo hareta
vasundharؤپل¹پ . ل¹£aل¹£ل¹­ivaruل¹£asahasrؤپل¹‡i viل¹£ل¹­hؤپyؤپل¹پ jؤپyate kل¹›miل¸¥ . kalpakoل¹­isahasrؤپل¹‡i
svarge modati bhإ«midaل¸¥ . Aؤ€kل¹£eptؤپ cؤپnumantؤپ ca tؤپnyeva narake vasetآ· . rؤپmeل¹‡ؤپpyu
ktaل¹پ . sarvvؤپnevaل¹پ bhؤپvinaل¸¥ pؤپrtthivendrؤپnآ· bhإ«yo bhإ«yo yؤپcate rؤپmabhadraل¸¥ . sؤپmؤپnyoyaل¹پ dharmmase
turnل¹›pؤپل¹‡ؤپل¹پ kؤپle kؤپle pؤپlanؤ«yo bhavadbhiل¸¥ . narendramل¹›garؤپjasya bhrؤپtaؤپ haihayavaل¹پإ›ajaل¸¥ . ؤ€jأ±apti
rasya dharmmasya rnل¹›parudranل¹›ipottamaل¸¥ . vijayavؤپdavؤپstavyؤپyaena Akل¹£aralalitؤپcؤپryyeل¹‡a likhitaل¹پ .

Hail ! The grandson of the illustrious Vijayؤپditya-mahؤپrؤپja, who was adorned with a multitude of blameless and noble virtues, who had acquired the splendour of victory by his own arm in many warlike encounters, and who adorned the race of the glorious Chaل¸·ukyas, who belong to the gotra of the Mؤپnavyas, who are praised in the whole world; who are the sons of Hؤپritؤ«; who have acquired the kingdom by the favour of (إڑiva) the husband of Kauإ›ikؤ«; who are protected by the assemblage of (divine) mothers; who are meditating at the feet of the lord Mahؤپsena (Skanda); who have subdued the territories of their enemies in an instant at the (mere) sight of the sign of the boar, a boon which they had obtained through the favour of the blessed Nؤپrؤپyaل¹‡a (Vishل¹‡u); and whose bodies are purified by bathing at the end of horse-sacrifices;â€”

The beloved son of Vishل¹‡uvardhana-mahؤپrؤپja, who subdued the surrounding territories of his enemies with the edge of his flashing sword, who surpassed by his deeds Nل¹›iga, Nala, Nahusha, Ambarؤ«sha and Yayؤپti, and who rejoiced in his sovereignty, just as Vishل¹‡u in his discus;â€”

He whose commands are cherished by the diadems of all vassals who bow affectionately to (his) majesty, who is a receptacle of the three powersThe three إ›aktis are prabhؤپva, utsؤپha and mantra. which are suitable to the warrior-caste, who is resplendent with the strength which he has acquired by victories in many battles, Narendra-mل¹›igarؤپja, who has cut the temples of the elephants of his foes with the sword (that resembles) a clawThe king's sword is compared to a claw and consequently Narendra-mل¹›igarؤپja (i.e., the lion among princes) himself to a lion or to Narasiل¹پha. in his arm which is as fierce as Yama's rod, who has subdued the six (internal) enemies,These are kؤپma, krodha, lobha, harsha, mؤپna and mada. who has acquired the four branches of royal science, who knows how to employ the four expedients,The four upؤپyas are sؤپman, dؤپna, bheda and deل¹‡ل¸چa. who chastises the wicked and shows favour to the good, who has conquered the world by his valour, just as (Vishل¹‡u) the destroyer of Madhu by his (three) stops, who is assisted by terrible and splendid courage, just as Yudhishل¹­hira was assisted by the courage of Bhؤ«ma and Arjuna, who, just as (Rؤپma) the son of Daإ›aratha, gives pleasure to Sؤ«tؤپ,In the case of the king, this seems to mean, that he was a favourite of Lakshmؤ«, with whom Sؤ«tؤپ is identified. who knows the right course, just as Manu, who disperses the crowd of his foes, just as the sun disperses the mass of darkness in a lotus-group,As â€œthe lotus-groupâ€‌ does not suit the context, I suspect that something has fallen out after padmؤپkara iva. the most pious one, the devout worshipper of Maheإ›vara (إڑiva), the asylum of the whole world, the illustrious Vijayؤپditya, the king of great kings, the supreme master, the lord, thus issues his commands to all householders, (viz.) heads of provinces, etc., who inhabit the district of . . . . .

(Line 17.) â€œBe it known to you, (that I) gave on the occasion of a lunar eclipse, with a libation of water, the village called Koل¹ںل¹ںapaل¹ںل¹ںu, making (it) exempt from all taxes, to (the following) twenty-four brؤپhmaل¹‡as, who are engaged in (the study of) the Vedas and Vedؤپل¹…gas and intent on (the performance of) the six duties, (viz.) to Veل¹‡ama-إ›arman, who dwells at Abhipendaل¹‡ل¸چuru, belongs to the Kauإ›ika-gotra, follows the Hiraل¹‡yakeإ›i-sإ«tra and is well versed in the Vedas and Vedؤپل¹…gas; to Chaل¹­i-إ›arman, who dwells at Nalإ«cheri, belongs to the Kauإ›ika-gotra and follows the Hiraل¹‡yakeإ›i-sإ«tra; to Vida-إ›arman, who dwells at Podeل¹…gu, belongs to the Gautama-gotra and follows the Hiraل¹‡yakeإ›i-sإ«tra; to Maviل¹‡ل¸چi-إ›arman, who dwells at Podaل¹…gu,This place is elsewhere called Podeل¹…gu. belongs to the Saل¹پkل¹›iti-gotra and follows the Hiraل¹‡yakeإ›i-sإ«tra; to Yؤپjأ±a-إ›arman, who dwells at Podeل¹…gu, belongs to the Harita-gotra and follows the Hiraل¹‡yakeإ›i-sإ«tra; to . . إ›arman, who dwells at Podeل¹…gu, belongs to the Saل¹پkل¹›iti-gotra and follows the Hiraل¹‡yakeإ›i-sإ«tra; to Kؤپma-إ›arman, who dwells at Krovؤپإ›iri,Elsewhere called Krovaإ›iri. belongs to the Harita-gotra and follows the Apastamba-sإ«tra; to Vishل¹‡u-إ›arman, who dwells at Urpuل¹­إ«ru, belongs to the Bhؤپradvؤپja-gotra and follows the Apastamba-sإ«tra; to Guأ±jadeva-إ›arman, who dwells at Vaل¹…gipaل¹ںل¹ںu, belongs to the Kauل¹‡ل¸چinya-gotra and follows the Apastamba-sإ«tra; to Bhadra-إ›arman, who dwells at Vaل¹…gipaل¹ںل¹ںu, belongs to the إڑؤپل¹‡ل¸چilya-gotra and follows the Apastamba-sإ«tra; to Vishل¹‡u-إ›arman, who dwells at Vaل¹…gipaل¹ںل¹ںu , belongs to the Kauل¹‡ل¸چinya-gotra and follows the Apastamba-sإ«tra; to Nؤپrؤپyaل¹‡a-إ›arman, who dwells at Vaل¹…gipaل¹ںل¹ںu, belongs to the Kauل¹‡ل¸چinya-gotra and follows the Apastamba-sإ«tra; to Droل¹‡a-إ›arman, who dwells at Chؤپnturu, belongs to the Bhؤپradvؤپja-gotra and follows the Apastamba-sإ«tra; to Nؤپrؤپyaل¹‡a-إ›arman, who dwells at Chؤپnturu, belongs to the Bhؤپradvؤپja-gotra and follows the ؤ€pastamba-sإ«tra; to Mؤپdhava-إ›arman, who dwells at Krovaإ›iri, belongs to the Harita-gotra and follows the ؤ€pastamba-sإ«tra; to Vennama-إ›arman, who dwells at Krovaإ›iri, belongs to the Parؤپإ›ara-gotra and follows the Apastamba-sإ«tra; to Arudi-إ›arman, who dwells at Krovaإ›iri, belongs to the Vatsa-gotra and follows the Apastamba-sإ«tra; to Nandi-إ›arman, who dwells at Urpuل¹­إ«ru, belongs to the Bhؤپradvؤپja-gotra and follows the Apastamba-sإ«tra; to Vishل¹‡u-إ›arman, who dwells at Kؤپrahaidu, belongs to the Bhؤپradvؤپja-gotra and follows the Apastamba-sإ«tra; to Bhؤپramؤپ-إ›arman, who dwells at Kؤپrahaidu, belongs to the Bhؤپradvؤپja-gotra and follows the Apastamba-sإ«tra; to Yaأ±ja-إ›arman, who dwells at Kؤپrahaidu, belongs to the Bhؤپradvؤپja-gotra and follows the Apastamba-sإ«tra; to Bؤپdadi-إ›arman, who dwells at Chؤپnturu, belongs to the Bhؤپradvؤپja-gotra and follows the Apastamba-sإ«tra; to Veل¹‡ل¹‡a-إ›arman, who dwells at Krؤپja, belongs to the Kauل¹‡ل¸چinya-gotra and follows the Apastamba-sإ«tra; and to ل¹‍ompaya-إ›arman, who dwells at Rؤپyإ«ru, belongs to the Agniveإ›ya-gotra and follows the â€œApastamba-sإ«tra.â€‌

(Line 43.) (There follows) the description of the boundaries of this (village.)This is left out in the translation, because the reading of the proper names is very uncertain owing to the bad preservation of this part of the grant. . . . . . . . . .

(Line 45.) Nobody shall cause obstruction to this (grant); he, who does it, becomes possessed of the five great sins. Vyؤپsa also has said: [Here follow three of the customary imprecatory verses, which it is unnecessary to translate.]
(Line 49.) Rؤپma also has said: [Here follows another verse.]
(Line 51.) The executor (ؤپjأ±apti) of this charity (was) the excellent prince Nل¹›ipa-Rudra, who was the brother of Narendra-mل¹›igarؤپja and a descendant of the Haihaya race.
(Line 52.) (This edict) was written by Aksharalalitؤپchؤپrya, who dwelt at Vijayavؤپda.
Digital edition of SII 1.35 by converted to DHARMA conventions by Emmanuel Francis.
See also edition by Daniel Balogh (Koل¹ںل¹ںapaل¹ںل¹ںu grant of Vijayؤپditya II ).
